obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be resilient against attempts by unauthorised third parties to alter their use, outputs or performance by exploiting system vulnerabilities. The technical solutions to address AI specific vulnerabilities shall include, where appropriate, measures to prevent, detect, respond to, resolve and control for attacks trying to manipulate the training data set (data poisoning), or pre-trained components used in training (model poisoning), inputs designed to cause the AI model to make a mistake (adversarial examples or model evasion), confidentiality attacks or model flaws.
scoring_text: Skill in AI-specific cybersecurity, including defense against data poisoning, model poisoning, adversarial examples, model evasion, confidentiality attacks, and model flaw exploitation — encompassing prevention, detection, response, resolution, and control measures for AI system vulnerabilities.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI-specific cybersecurity, including defense against data poisoning, model poisoning, adversarial examples, model evasion, confidentiality attacks, and model flaw exploitation — encompassing prevention, detection, response, resolution, and control measures for AI system vulnerabilities.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI security assessments, including threat modeling for AI systems, attack surface analysis, and control gap identification.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 15(4) (obligation) equal WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.824, cosine: 0.779). Secondary: RM-K-005 (RM, cosine: 0.697), SP-S-008 (SP, cosine: 0.686).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 8.2/10 (raw 0.824). Shared vocabulary: [ai, control, skill]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [adversarial, against, attacks, confidentiality, cybersecurity, data] vs WIDAI terms [analysis, assessments, attack, conducting, gap, identification] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.892 (P=0.873, R=0.911). R exceeds P by 0.038 — the WIDAI KSA's 19 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 34 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: notable entailment (21.8%). "Skill in AI-specific cybersecurity, including defense against data poisoning, mo" shows logical implication toward "Skill in conducting AI security assessments, including threat modeling for AI sy" — stronger than typical for cross-framework competency pairs, reinforcing the STS-based relationship.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.779 ≈ STS 0.824 (gap 0.044). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, control, skill] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, control, skill]. EU AI Act-only: [adversarial, against, attacks, confidentiality, cybersecurity, data]. WIDAI-only: [analysis, assessments, attack, conducting, gap, identification].
